Cumulative Gains % positives captured

Captures what fraction of frauds are in the top x% by score. Orange dot = operating point.

Lift Curve vs random baseline

At each score percentile: precision ÷ overall positive rate. Orange dot = operating point.

Calibration reliability diagram

Dashed = perfect calibration. Model slightly under-predicts in 0.25–0.45 range; well-calibrated above 0.5.

Global Explainability

TreeSHAP & Permutation Importance · run-009
Feature Importance TreeSHAP Permutation
Increases score Decreases score (SHAP)
TreeSHAP (left): mean |SHAP value| per feature — exact Shapley values from the tree structure. Order-independent and handles interactions.  Permutation (right): drop in AUC when a feature is randomly shuffled — model-agnostic and measures true predictive contribution.
TreeSHAP — mean |SHAP value|
Permutation — AUC drop
Partial Dependence (PDP) & Individual Conditional Expectation (ICE) all features
PDP: average model response as each feature sweeps its range (all other features marginalised). Bold line = population average.  ICE: one line per sampled instance — reveals heterogeneity hidden by the PDP average. Orange dashed = reference observation value.
txn_velocity_1h
merchant_risk_score
geo_anomaly_flag
amt_z_score
device_fingerprint_match
ip_trust_score
account_age_days
card_age_days
Feature Interactions H-stat ⓘ Friedman H-statistic
Measures the fraction of the joint partial dependence variance of two features that is attributable to their interaction (not their individual effects). H=0 means no interaction; H≈1 means the joint effect is almost entirely interaction-driven.

Friedman & Popescu, 2008 — "Predictive Learning via Rule Ensembles"
Higher H = stronger non-additive interaction between the two features.

Case Explanation — select a row above to update
TXN-31042 False Positive Actual: Legit Decision: BLOCKED $8,412 Top driver: txn_velocity_1h  (increased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.142 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.921. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
This corporate account executed 47 transactions in a single hour — a payroll-batch pattern the model cannot distinguish from velocity fraud. The payroll processor merchant carries risk_score 0.71 due to category classification alone. Both signals combined push the score to 0.921. A licensed-payroll-processor whitelist rule would suppress this class of false positive.
What would change this decision?
FeatureCurrentTargetImpact
txn_velocity_1h47.0 tx/hr→ < 15 tx/hr−0.22 → score ≈ 0.70 (REVIEW)
merchant_risk_score0.71→ < 0.45−0.14 → score ≈ 0.78 (REVIEW)
TXN-29841 False Positive Actual: Legit Decision: BLOCKED $1,921 Top driver: geo_anomaly_flag  (increased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.142 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.887. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
Customer used a new device while abroad (geo_anomaly 0.92) on a business trip. The transaction covers a hotel invoice — a legitimate cross-border purchase. Without traveler-mode context, the model treats this as suspicious. A pre-trip travel flag in the customer profile or a separate geo-trust module would help.
What would change this decision?
FeatureCurrentTargetImpact
geo_anomaly_flag0.92→ < 0.30−0.19 → score ≈ 0.70 (REVIEW)
device_fingerprint0.41→ < 0.20−0.09 → score ≈ 0.80 (REVIEW)
TXN-18920 False Positive Actual: Legit Decision: REVIEW $641 Top driver: geo_anomaly_flag  (increased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.142 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.874. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
A recent phone upgrade created a new device fingerprint (anomaly 0.71) combined with a mild geo anomaly from a different home-network IP. No single driver dominates — all five features contribute positively, leaving the model uncertain. Device-change events logged by customer service should open a 48-hour trust window.
What would change this decision?
FeatureCurrentTargetImpact
device_fingerprint0.71→ < 0.25−0.12 → score ≈ 0.75 (REVIEW)
geo_anomaly_flag0.78→ < 0.30−0.12 → score ≈ 0.75 (REVIEW)
TXN-44219 False Positive Actual: Legit Decision: BLOCKED $2,840 Top driver: merchant_risk_score  (increased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.142 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.862. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
A retail crypto investor making a legitimate on-ramp purchase at a licensed exchange. The merchant risk_score (0.81) is driven entirely by category, not conduct. Merchant attribution drives 41% of the total SHAP value. Crypto on-ramp transactions above KYC verification tier could be conditionally allowed.
What would change this decision?
FeatureCurrentTargetImpact
merchant_risk_score0.81→ < 0.45−0.25 → score ≈ 0.61 (APPROVED)
TXN-08811 False Positive Actual: Legit Decision: REVIEW $411 Top driver: amt_z_score  (increased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.142 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.851. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
A high-value jewelry purchase (amt_z_score 4.9) from a newly enrolled device triggered REVIEW. Customer history shows periodic large gift purchases on anniversary dates — this is a known pattern in the account. Per-customer z-score normalisation using 12-month history would reduce false-positive rate.
What would change this decision?
FeatureCurrentTargetImpact
amt_z_score4.9→ < 2.0−0.19 → score ≈ 0.66 (APPROVED)
device_fingerprint0.65→ < 0.30−0.09 → score ≈ 0.76 (REVIEW)
TXN-88291 True Positive Actual: Fraud Decision: BLOCKED $4,820 Top driver: txn_velocity_1h  (increased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.142 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.972. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
Classic card-testing → large-purchase pattern. 78 micro-test charges per hour across multiple merchants preceded a $4,820 electronics buy. Velocity and merchant risk dominate. The familiar device fingerprint (0.08) indicates a compromised card rather than account takeover — the attacker kept the original device to stay under radar.
What would change this decision?
No single-feature counterfactual would flip this decision — the risk signal is strong across multiple features.
TXN-72841 True Positive Actual: Fraud Decision: BLOCKED $1,247 Top driver: geo_anomaly_flag  (increased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.142 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.956. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
Stolen card used abroad — 2,400 km from the cardholder's home city. Geo anomaly (0.95) is the dominant SHAP contributor, accounting for 36% of total attribution. The merchant is a hotel (elevated risk category). Cardholder confirmed they did not travel and did not initiate this charge.
What would change this decision?
No single-feature counterfactual would flip this decision — the risk signal is strong across multiple features.
TXN-91182 True Positive Actual: Fraud Decision: BLOCKED $892 Top driver: geo_anomaly_flag  (increased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.142 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.943. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
Compromised credentials used from an overseas IP. Geo anomaly (0.91) and merchant risk (0.66) are the two primary SHAP contributors. Velocity is deliberately moderate — only 6 tx/hr — as the attacker attempted to avoid velocity-based rules. The ring chart shows geo dominates 42% of attribution.
What would change this decision?
No single-feature counterfactual would flip this decision — the risk signal is strong across multiple features.
TXN-66203 True Positive Actual: Fraud Decision: REVIEW $3,102 Top driver: txn_velocity_1h  (increased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.142 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.921. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
Account-takeover via credential stuffing. Velocity (32/hr) and a new device fingerprint (0.68) are top drivers. The geo anomaly is moderate — attacker used a domestic proxy to mimic the cardholder's region. REVIEW decision was appropriate; manual review confirmed fraud within 90 minutes.
What would change this decision?
No single-feature counterfactual would flip this decision — the risk signal is strong across multiple features.
TXN-55019 True Positive Actual: Fraud Decision: BLOCKED $281 Top driver: amt_z_score  (increased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.142 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.904. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
Test-charge escalation attack: 28 micro-charges (avg $2.10) were run to verify card validity before a $281 semi-large purchase attempt. Amount z-score (4.2) and velocity combine for the top-two SHAP attributions. Geo is mild — a domestic attacker using the cardholder's carrier network.
What would change this decision?
No single-feature counterfactual would flip this decision — the risk signal is strong across multiple features.
TXN-77291 False Negative Actual: Fraud Decision: APPROVED $88 Top driver: merchant_risk_score  (increased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.142 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.412. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
Low-and-slow fraud: the fraudster used the stolen card patiently — 3 tx/hr, domestic location, familiar device. Only merchant risk (0.48) and a small amount anomaly provide signal. The low device-fingerprint score actively suppresses the overall score. Behavioural time-series features or merchant-network graph features may surface this pattern.
What would change this decision?
FeatureCurrentTargetImpact
merchant_risk_score0.48→ > 0.65would raise score to ≈ 0.54 (still APPROVED)
TXN-63018 False Negative Actual: Fraud Decision: APPROVED $42 Top driver: merchant_risk_score  (increased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.142 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.387. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
Insider fraud: account holder's family member used a known household device. The device_fingerprint anomaly is near-zero (0.05), actively reducing the score. Merchant risk (0.61) is the only meaningful positive signal. Shared-device flagging and relationship-network features are critical to catch this.
What would change this decision?
Insufficient signal for counterfactual — model score is near baseline.
TXN-51204 False Negative Actual: Fraud Decision: APPROVED $31 Top driver: txn_velocity_1h  (increased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.142 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.361. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
Account farming: the fraudster maintained a dormant account for months before beginning small extractions. Small transaction amounts (negative amt_z_score contribution) actively suppress the model score. Account-age and cumulative-extraction velocity features would help surface this pattern.
What would change this decision?
Insufficient signal for counterfactual — model score is near baseline.
TXN-29820 False Negative Actual: Fraud Decision: APPROVED $19 Top driver: merchant_risk_score  (increased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.142 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.334. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
Synthetic identity fraud: a manufactured credit profile with a groomed transaction history. No individual feature is anomalous — score sits just 0.19 above the population baseline. The model has no strong signal to flag. Identity-graph linkage and bureau thin-file detection are required.
What would change this decision?
Insufficient signal for counterfactual — model score is near baseline.
TXN-14091 False Negative Actual: Fraud Decision: APPROVED $11 Top driver: merchant_risk_score  (increased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.142 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.298. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
Money-mule outbound transfer: an otherwise legitimate-looking account used to pass fraudulent funds downstream. This outbound transaction appears entirely normal — all features below the population median. Network-based features (who sent funds INTO this account?) are critical to detect mule accounts.
What would change this decision?
Insufficient signal for counterfactual — model score is near baseline.
TXN-02401 True Negative Actual: Legit Decision: APPROVED $62 Top driver: merchant_risk_score  (decreased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.142 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.041. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
Regular grocery purchase at a trusted neighbourhood merchant. All features except velocity suppress the score below baseline. Low merchant risk and zero geo anomaly are the strongest negative drivers. This is the model working as intended.
What would change this decision?
No single-feature counterfactual would flip this decision — the risk signal is strong across multiple features.
TXN-03812 True Negative Actual: Legit Decision: APPROVED $84 Top driver: merchant_risk_score  (decreased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.142 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.038. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
Recurring utility autopay from a long-standing account. Device and merchant are both highly familiar. Score is near-zero, reflecting maximum model confidence in legitimacy. All four negative-SHAP features combine to nearly cancel the baseline.
What would change this decision?
No single-feature counterfactual would flip this decision — the risk signal is strong across multiple features.
TXN-04291 True Negative Actual: Legit Decision: APPROVED $124 Top driver: merchant_risk_score  (decreased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.142 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.031. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
Brand-loyal repeat customer — 6th purchase at the same merchant this month. All five SHAP contributions are negative (score-suppressing). Merchant risk (0.09) is in the bottom decile of the portfolio. Perfect true negative.
What would change this decision?
No single-feature counterfactual would flip this decision — the risk signal is strong across multiple features.
TXN-05120 True Negative Actual: Legit Decision: APPROVED $208 Top driver: merchant_risk_score  (decreased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.142 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.029. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
Streaming subscription upgrade — slightly above-average amount (z=1.4) for a plan tier change, but all risk signals are clean. The positive velocity contribution is negligible. A confident APPROVED with near-zero fraud probability.
What would change this decision?
No single-feature counterfactual would flip this decision — the risk signal is strong across multiple features.
TXN-06041 True Negative Actual: Legit Decision: APPROVED $45 Top driver: merchant_risk_score  (decreased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.142 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.021. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
ATM cash withdrawal — the lowest model score in the sample set. Known ATM operator (risk 0.08), home city, familiar device, below-average amount. Every feature suppresses the score strongly. Textbook true negative.
What would change this decision?
No single-feature counterfactual would flip this decision — the risk signal is strong across multiple features.

Endpoints & Schemas

POST/v1/predict

Score a single transaction in real-time. Returns fraud probability, binary decision, and rule triggers.

P50: 12msP99: 47msSLO: 100ms
Request
{"transaction_id": "txn_xyz",
 "amount": 247.50,
 "merchant_id": "M8821",
 "account_id": "A44291",
 "timestamp": "2026-03-24T14:22:01Z"}
Response
{"score": 0.847,
 "decision": "REVIEW",
 "threshold": 0.70,
 "model_version": "run-009",
 "latency_ms": 14}
POST/v1/explain

Return SHAP feature attributions, human-readable narrative, and full rule evaluation trace — which rules were evaluated, which fired, and which set the final outcome.

P50: 38msP99: 142msSLO: 250ms
Request
{"transaction_id": "txn_xyz",
 "include_narrative": true,
 "include_rules": true,
 "top_k": 5}
Response
{"score": 0.847,
 "shap_values": {"txn_velocity_1h": 0.31,
   "merchant_risk_score": 0.24, ...},
 "base_value": 0.142,
 "top_drivers": ["txn_velocity_1h",
   "merchant_risk_score"],
 "narrative": "High velocity (9 txn/hr)...",
 "rule_trace": {
   "rules_evaluated": 6,
   "rules_fired": [
     {"id": "rule-4", "name": "Moderate review",
      "condition": "score >= 0.55 AND score < 0.80",
      "outcome": "REVIEW", "order": 4},
     {"id": "rule-5", "name": "High-risk block",
      "condition": "score >= 0.80 AND amount > 500",
      "outcome": "BLOCK", "order": 5}],
   "last_fired": "rule-5",
   "final_outcome": "BLOCK",
   "fallback_used": false
 }}
POST/v1/rules/evaluate

Evaluate the active rule set against a pre-scored record. Returns a complete rule-fire trace without re-invoking the ML model. Useful for auditing, appeals, and rule debugging.

P50: 4msP99: 11msSLO: 50ms
Request
{"transaction_id": "txn_xyz",
 "score": 0.847,
 "features": {
   "amount": 247.50,
   "merchant_category": "crypto",
   "velocity_24h": 9,
 "rule_set_version": "v14"}
Response
{"rule_set_version": "v14",
 "rules_evaluated": 6,
 "trace": [
   {"rule": "rule-1", "fired": false,
    "reason": "score 0.847 ≥ 0.40"},
   {"rule": "rule-4", "fired": true,
    "condition_met": "score in [0.55,0.80)",
    "outcome": "REVIEW"},
   {"rule": "rule-5", "fired": true,
    "condition_met": "score≥0.80 ∧ amt>500",
    "outcome": "BLOCK"}],
 "final_outcome": "BLOCK",
 "last_fired_rule": "rule-5",
 "fallback_used": false,
 "eval_ms": 3}
POST/v1/label

Submit analyst ground-truth labels and feedback. Feeds the active learning loop.

Labels today: 47Loop lag: 6hCoverage: 78%
Request
{"transaction_id": "txn_xyz",
 "label": "fraud",
 "analyst_id": "ana_001",
 "confidence": "high",
 "notes": "Card-present anomaly"}
POST/v1/predict/batch

Async batch scoring. Returns a job_id; poll /v1/jobs/{id} for results.

Max rows: 500kAvg time: 8 min
Request
{"gcs_uri": "gs://bucket/txns.parquet",
 "output_uri": "gs://bucket/scores/",
 "notify_email": "team@jha.com"}
GET/v1/health

Liveness and readiness probe. Returns model version, uptime, and dependency status.

Uptime: 99.97%Last deploy: 4d ago
Response
{"status": "healthy",
 "model": "run-009",
 "uptime_s": 345600,
 "deps": {"bq": "ok", "fs": "ok"}}
Supporting Tables (hosting configured at deployment time)
🗄predictionsPARTITIONED scored_at
ColumnTypeDescription
transaction_idSTRINGSource transaction ID
scoreFLOAT64Model fraud probability 0–1
decisionSTRINGBLOCKED / REVIEW / APPROVE
model_versionSTRINGML platform run ID
scored_atTIMESTAMPPartition column
latency_msINT64Inference latency
🔍explanationsAuto-explain REVIEW/BLOCK
ColumnTypeDescription
transaction_idSTRINGFK → predictions
shap_valuesJSONFeature → SHAP value map
top_driversARRAY<STRING>Top-5 feature names
narrativeSTRINGLLM-generated explanation
base_valueFLOAT64SHAP base (population avg)
rule_traceJSONRules evaluated / fired / final
last_fired_ruleSTRINGRule ID that set final outcome
explained_atTIMESTAMPPartition column
🏷labelsFeedback loop
ColumnTypeDescription
transaction_idSTRINGFK → predictions
labelSTRINGfraud / legit / unknown
analyst_idSTRINGReviewing analyst
confidenceSTRINGhigh / medium / low
notesSTRINGFree-text analyst notes
labeled_atTIMESTAMPPartition column

TXN-782912710:42:18$12,500Wire IntlWireunusual_amount (+0.45)
0.97
CriticalBLOCKED▶
0.97 Critical BLOCKED top: unusual_amount
SHAP Explanation
Narrative

Wire transfer to international beneficiary flagged critical. The amount ($12,500) is 14x the member's 30-day average. Geo-velocity alert: last transaction 1,400 miles away 3 hours prior. New device fingerprint. Combined signal drives a 97% fraud probability — automatic block applied.

Network Graph
Drift Detail —
Distribution Shift (12 weeks)
PSI crossed warning threshold (0.05) in week 7. Investigate the underlying data pipeline for changes around that date.
Reference vs Current Distribution
Blue = training distribution. Orange = production (last 7 days). Shift indicates merchants in a new risk tier have increased volume.
Recommended Actions
merchant_risk_scorePSI = 0.071 — above warning threshold. Re-check merchant risk scoring pipeline for recent changes.
RecalibrationScore distribution has shifted. Consider re-calibrating the model on recent production data.

Override Patterns Last 7 days · 13 overrides
PatternCountLikely Cause
Model: BLOCK → Analyst: APPROVE 7 Rule over-triggered on amount threshold
Model: REVIEW → Analyst: BLOCK 4 Late-stage velocity pattern missed
Rule: REVIEW → Analyst: APPROVE 6 Merchant known to analyst but not model
Rule: BLOCK → Analyst: REVIEW 2 Score borderline, context clears

Overrides are logged and fed back into the active learning loop. Recurring patterns are surfaced as rule optimization candidates in the Decision section.

Feedback Loop

How analyst decisions improve the model
312
Labels This Week
ground truth collected

Est. AUC Lift
from this batch
500 labels
Retrain Trigger
62% reached
14%
Disagreement Rate
analyst vs model
Disagreement Analysis by Score Band Where analysts override model/rules
Score BandCases Disagreements RatePattern
0.55–0.65 43 19 44% Model uncertain; analyst uses context
0.65–0.75 61 11 18% Rules fire REVIEW; some cleared
0.75–0.85 58 8 14% High-score range; most confirmed fraud
0.85–0.95 72 3 4% Very high score; analysts rarely override

Cases where analyst decision differs from the rule engine final outcome. High disagreement in the 0.55–0.65 band suggests the model uncertainty region may benefit from a new rule or score recalibration.

Ground Truth Pipeline
Analyst labels case (ground truth)
Stored in predictions table with analyst_label field
Label buffer accumulates (312/500)
Retrain triggered at 500 new labels or drift alert
Challenger model trained on updated dataset
Auto-registered as new experiment run
Challenger evaluated vs champion
Promoted if AUC lift > 0.005 with p < 0.05
Agreement Rate (12 weeks)

Weekly analyst–model agreement rate. Declining trend may indicate model drift.

Label Volume (12 weeks)

Cases labeled per week. Dips may reflect analyst capacity or queue throttling.

Retrain Progress
312 / 500
Est. days to trigger4 days
Last retrain18 days ago
Est. AUC after retrain0.948

Outcomes

Configure decision labels for this project
⚙
Sequential Rule Engine — Last-Fired-Wins
All rules are evaluated in order. The outcome set by the last rule whose condition is true becomes the final decision. If no rules fire, the fallback outcome is used.
FALLBACK
REVIEW
APPROVE
Transaction is approved automatically. No further review required.
Human review requiredNo
Currently in queue0
REVIEW FALLBACK
Transaction is flagged for analyst review before settlement.
Human review requiredYes
Currently in queue34
BLOCK
Transaction is declined immediately. Customer notified.
Human review requiredNo
Currently in queue0
ESCALATE
High-risk case escalated to senior fraud analyst.
Human review requiredYes
Currently in queue4
Outcome Routing
OutcomeTriggers Human ReviewNotifies CustomerLogged to PredictionsWebhook
APPROVENoYesYes—
REVIEWYes — Ana. QueueNoYes—
BLOCKNoYes — declineYesfraud-alert
ESCALATEYes — SeniorNoYesescalation

Rule Editor

Sequential engine · last-fired-wins
How it works: All rules are evaluated top-to-bottom on every prediction. Each rule that fires sets the outcome. The last rule to fire is the final decision. Drag rows to reorder. Rules with lower priority (higher number) override earlier ones.
Active Rules 6 rules · evaluated in order
Order Name Condition Outcome Frequency Last Fired Status Actions
⠿ 1 Low-risk auto-approve fraud_score < 0.40 APPROVE 4,821/day last fired 2 s ago Active
⠿ 2 Crypto high-amount block fraud_score > 0.55 AND merchant_category = crypto AND amount > 2000 BLOCK 38/day last fired 4 min ago Active
⠿ 3 Velocity spike block velocity_24h > 12 AND fraud_score > 0.50 BLOCK 22/day last fired 11 min ago Active
⠿ 4 Moderate risk review fraud_score >= 0.55 AND fraud_score < 0.80 REVIEW 312/day last fired 8 s ago Active
⠿ 5 High-risk block fraud_score >= 0.80 AND amount > 500 BLOCK 91/day last fired 1 min ago Active
⠿ 6 Very high-risk escalate fraud_score >= 0.92 ESCALATE 18/day last fired 22 min ago Active
+ Add New Rule

Rule Firing Frequency

Daily firing count per rule. Rules firing rarely may be redundant; rules with high FP may need threshold tuning.

Accuracy by Rule
RuleFrequencyPrimary Outcome TP RateFP RateFN Rate
Low-risk approve 4821/day APPROVE
93%

0%
96%

TP = rule outcome matches ground truth. FP = rule fires but analyst/ground truth disagrees. FN = rule did not fire but should have.

Score × Outcome Heatmap

Distribution of final rule outcomes across model score bands. Darker cell = higher concentration.

🎯 Optimization Suggestions

Auto-generated by minimizing FP+FN on the evaluation dataset while preserving TP rate ≥ current baseline.

🔴 High — Moderate review rule (4) has 33% FP rate
💡 Raise lower threshold from 0.55 → 0.60; estimated −18% FP, −2% TP
🟡 Med — Velocity block (rule 3) fires rarely (22/day)
💡 Consider merging into rule 5 or raising velocity_24h threshold to 15
🟢 Low — Auto-approve cutoff at 0.40 is conservative
💡 Eval shows lowering to 0.35 adds 8% throughput with <0.5% TP loss

Cumulative Gains % positives captured

Captures what fraction of defaults are in the top x% by score. Orange dot = operating point.

Lift Curve vs random baseline

At each score percentile: precision ÷ overall positive rate. Orange dot = operating point.

Calibration reliability diagram

Dashed = perfect calibration. Model slightly under-predicts in 0.25–0.45 range; well-calibrated above 0.5.

Global Explainability

TreeSHAP & Permutation Importance · run-004
Feature Importance TreeSHAP Permutation
Increases score Decreases score (SHAP)
TreeSHAP (left): mean |SHAP value| per feature — exact Shapley values from the tree structure. Order-independent and handles interactions.  Permutation (right): drop in AUC when a feature is randomly shuffled — model-agnostic and measures true predictive contribution.
TreeSHAP — mean |SHAP value|
Permutation — AUC drop
Partial Dependence (PDP) & Individual Conditional Expectation (ICE) all features
PDP: average model response as each feature sweeps its range (all other features marginalised). Bold line = population average.  ICE: one line per sampled instance — reveals heterogeneity hidden by the PDP average. Orange dashed = reference observation value.
dti_ratio
payment_history_score
credit_utilization
income_stability_index
loan_age_months
employment_length_years
derogatory_marks
hard_inquiries_12m
Feature Interactions H-stat ⓘ Friedman H-statistic
Measures the fraction of the joint partial dependence variance of two features that is attributable to their interaction (not their individual effects). H=0 means no interaction; H≈1 means the joint effect is almost entirely interaction-driven.

Friedman & Popescu, 2008 — "Predictive Learning via Rule Ensembles"
Higher H = stronger non-additive interaction between the two features.

0.201

0.162
dti_ratio × credit_utilization
0.131
employment_length_years × derogatory_marks
0.088
Residual Diagnostics distribution
Histogram of (predicted score − actual label). Orange tails = systematic mis-prediction on edge segments.

Case Explanation — select a row above to update
LOAN-31042 False Positive Actual: Good Decision: BLOCKED $42,000 Top driver: dti_ratio  (increased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.128 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.900. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
Applicant has a high DTI ratio (0.61) driven by an existing mortgage with a zero-delinquency 8-year record. Payment history (0.48) appears low due to bureau data lag after a recently paid-off auto loan. Bureau-refresh lag correction and mortgage-specific DTI adjustment would reduce this FP.
What would change this decision?
FeatureCurrentTargetImpact
dti_ratio0.61→ < 0.40−0.24 → score ≈ 0.68 (REVIEW)
payment_history_score0.48→ > 0.65−0.14 → score ≈ 0.78 (REVIEW)
LOAN-29841 False Positive Actual: Good Decision: BLOCKED $28,500 Top driver: credit_utilization  (increased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.128 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.883. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
Credit utilization spiked to 78% after an emergency medical bill. Prior 24 months show utilization consistently under 25%. The model sees a point-in-time snapshot without context. 12-month utilization trend features would distinguish temporary vs chronic elevation.
What would change this decision?
FeatureCurrentTargetImpact
credit_utilization0.78→ < 0.35−0.19 → score ≈ 0.70 (REVIEW)
LOAN-18920 False Positive Actual: Good Decision: REVIEW $19,200 Top driver: credit_utilization  (increased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.128 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.860. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
Recent career change (1 year at new employer) reduces both employment_length and income_stability_index. This applicant left a 9-year corporate role for a 40%-higher-paying position. Static features cannot capture the income growth. Employment tenure combined with income growth delta would better signal upward mobility.
What would change this decision?
FeatureCurrentTargetImpact
employment_length_years1.0→ > 4.0−0.10 → score ≈ 0.77 (REVIEW)
income_stability_index0.52→ > 0.70−0.09 → score ≈ 0.78 (REVIEW)
LOAN-44219 False Positive Actual: Good Decision: BLOCKED $37,800 Top driver: payment_history_score  (increased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.128 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.848. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
Thin bureau file with only 2 credit accounts, both opened within 18 months. Payment history score (0.38) is low because of age-of-account weighting, not delinquencies. Income is stable (government employee, 7 years). Thin-file applicants need alternative data: rent payments, utilities, bank-cashflow history.
What would change this decision?
FeatureCurrentTargetImpact
payment_history_score0.38→ > 0.60−0.24 → score ≈ 0.62 (APPROVED)
LOAN-08811 False Positive Actual: Good Decision: REVIEW $12,400 Top driver: income_stability_index  (increased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.128 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.837. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
Self-employed applicant with seasonal income variability classified as low income_stability (0.31). Net income averages $82k over 3 years but shows expected seasonal patterns. Standard documentation misses the stable multi-year picture. Multi-year average income and seasonal adjustment improve stability scoring for self-employed.
What would change this decision?
FeatureCurrentTargetImpact
income_stability_index0.31→ > 0.55−0.18 → score ≈ 0.67 (APPROVED)
LOAN-88291 True Positive Actual: Default Decision: BLOCKED $48,000 Top driver: dti_ratio  (increased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.128 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.978. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
All four primary risk indicators are elevated: DTI 0.78, payment history 0.22, utilization 0.82, income stability 0.28. Applicant has two recent 60-day delinquencies and a charge-off in the prior 12 months. Employment tenure of 1 year. Model correctly blocks with highest confidence in the sample.
What would change this decision?
No single-feature counterfactual would flip this decision — the risk signal is strong across multiple features.

Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.128 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.942. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
Maxed-out credit (utilization 0.88) combined with a troubled payment history (0.31) and moderate DTI. Credit utilization is the dominant SHAP contributor (36%). Historical cohort analysis shows 73% default rate at this feature combination.
What would change this decision?
No single-feature counterfactual would flip this decision — the risk signal is strong across multiple features.

Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.128 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.929. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative

What would change this decision?
No single-feature counterfactual would flip this decision — the risk signal is strong across multiple features.

Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.128 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.890. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
Extremely low income stability (0.18) — gig-economy worker with highly volatile cash flow. Multiple income interruptions in 18 months. DTI (0.69) is calculated on peak income, but actual average income cannot service the proposed obligation.
What would change this decision?
No single-feature counterfactual would flip this decision — the risk signal is strong across multiple features.
LOAN-77291 False Negative Actual: Default Decision: APPROVED $4,200 Top driver: payment_history_score  (increased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.128 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.398. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
Strategic default: applicant had a genuinely strong profile at origination. Default occurred 18 months later after a sudden job loss. Features were legitimately favorable at application time. Macroeconomic stress scenarios and industry-sector risk features may add leading-indicator signal.
What would change this decision?
FeatureCurrentTargetImpact
payment_history_score0.52→ > 0.70would have raised score to ≈ 0.50
LOAN-63018 False Negative Actual: Default Decision: APPROVED $2,800 Top driver: payment_history_score  (increased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.128 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.373. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
Hidden co-borrower obligations: applicant had co-signed two other loans that did not appear on the bureau file. When those loans went delinquent, this applicant followed. Co-borrower network checks and undisclosed-obligation flags are essential.
What would change this decision?
Insufficient signal for counterfactual — model score is near baseline.
LOAN-51204 False Negative Actual: Default Decision: APPROVED $5,100 Top driver: dti_ratio  (increased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.128 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.347. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
Medical debt exclusion timing: a $45k medical bill appeared on the bureau 4 months after loan origination. Utilization was clean (0.21) at application. The post-disbursement burden proved insurmountable. Soft medical-debt inquiry flags from data partners may surface pre-bureau risk.
What would change this decision?
Insufficient signal for counterfactual — model score is near baseline.
LOAN-29820 False Negative Actual: Default Decision: APPROVED $3,600 Top driver: payment_history_score  (increased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.128 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.320. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
Divorce-related income shock: applicant's income halved after separation — undisclosed at application. The model had no way to anticipate this life-event change. Income re-verification at 6-month post-origination and marital-status-change alerts recommended.
What would change this decision?
Insufficient signal for counterfactual — model score is near baseline.
LOAN-14091 False Negative Actual: Default Decision: APPROVED $1,900 Top driver: payment_history_score  (increased risk)
Risk Score
SHAP Attribution
Feature vs Population IQR
Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.128 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.284. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
Small loan, high relative burden: the $1,900 emergency car-repair loan appeared manageable in aggregate. But the applicant's residual discretionary income after all existing obligations was near zero. Residual-income features (after-obligation disposable cash) are stronger predictors for small loans.
What would change this decision?
Insufficient signal for counterfactual — model score is near baseline.

Feature Contributions (SHAP Waterfall)
E[f(x)] = 0.128 (population baseline, shown as dashed line). f(x) = 0.027. ■ Orange = increases score   ■ Blue = decreases score
Explanation Narrative
Prime borrower: low DTI, excellent 12-year payment history (0.88), minimal utilization, stable income, long tenure. All four risk features suppress the score. Score is well below any threshold — textbook TN approval.

No single-feature counterfactual would flip this decision — the risk signal is strong across multiple features.

Endpoints & Schemas

POST/v1/predict

Score a single transaction in real-time. Returns fraud probability, binary decision, and rule triggers.

P50: 12msP99: 47msSLO: 100ms
Request
{"transaction_id": "txn_xyz",
 "amount": 247.50,
 "merchant_id": "M8821",
 "account_id": "A44291",
 "timestamp": "2026-03-24T14:22:01Z"}
Response
{"score": 0.847,
 "decision": "REVIEW",
 "threshold": 0.70,
 "model_version": "run-009",
 "latency_ms": 14}
POST/v1/explain

Return SHAP feature attributions, human-readable narrative, and full rule evaluation trace — which rules were evaluated, which fired, and which set the final outcome.

P50: 38msP99: 142msSLO: 250ms
Request
{"transaction_id": "txn_xyz",
 "include_narrative": true,
 "include_rules": true,
 "top_k": 5}
Response
{"score": 0.847,
 "shap_values": {"txn_velocity_1h": 0.31,
   "merchant_risk_score": 0.24, ...},
 "base_value": 0.142,
 "top_drivers": ["txn_velocity_1h",
   "merchant_risk_score"],
 "narrative": "High velocity (9 txn/hr)...",
 "rule_trace": {
   "rules_evaluated": 6,
   "rules_fired": [
     {"id": "rule-4", "name": "Moderate review",
      "condition": "score >= 0.55 AND score < 0.80",
      "outcome": "REVIEW", "order": 4},
     {"id": "rule-5", "name": "High-risk block",
      "condition": "score >= 0.80 AND amount > 500",
      "outcome": "BLOCK", "order": 5}],
   "last_fired": "rule-5",
   "final_outcome": "BLOCK",
   "fallback_used": false
 }}
POST/v1/rules/evaluate

Evaluate the active rule set against a pre-scored record. Returns a complete rule-fire trace without re-invoking the ML model. Useful for auditing, appeals, and rule debugging.

P50: 4msP99: 11msSLO: 50ms
Request
{"transaction_id": "txn_xyz",
 "score": 0.847,
 "features": {
   "amount": 247.50,
   "merchant_category": "crypto",
   "velocity_24h": 9,
 "rule_set_version": "v14"}
Response
{"rule_set_version": "v14",
 "rules_evaluated": 6,
 "trace": [
   {"rule": "rule-1", "fired": false,
    "reason": "score 0.847 ≥ 0.40"},
   {"rule": "rule-4", "fired": true,
    "condition_met": "score in [0.55,0.80)",
    "outcome": "REVIEW"},
   {"rule": "rule-5", "fired": true,
    "condition_met": "score≥0.80 ∧ amt>500",
    "outcome": "BLOCK"}],
 "final_outcome": "BLOCK",
 "last_fired_rule": "rule-5",
 "fallback_used": false,
 "eval_ms": 3}
POST/v1/label

Submit analyst ground-truth labels and feedback. Feeds the active learning loop.

Labels today: 47Loop lag: 6hCoverage: 78%
Request
{"transaction_id": "txn_xyz",
 "label": "fraud",
 "analyst_id": "ana_001",
 "confidence": "high",
 "notes": "Card-present anomaly"}
POST/v1/predict/batch

Async batch scoring. Returns a job_id; poll /v1/jobs/{id} for results.

Max rows: 500kAvg time: 8 min
Request
{"gcs_uri": "gs://bucket/txns.parquet",
 "output_uri": "gs://bucket/scores/",
 "notify_email": "team@jha.com"}
GET/v1/health

Liveness and readiness probe. Returns model version, uptime, and dependency status.

Uptime: 99.97%Last deploy: 4d ago
Response
{"status": "healthy",
 "model": "run-009",
 "uptime_s": 345600,
 "deps": {"bq": "ok", "fs": "ok"}}
Supporting Tables (hosting configured at deployment time)
🗄predictionsPARTITIONED scored_at
ColumnTypeDescription
transaction_idSTRINGSource transaction ID
scoreFLOAT64Model fraud probability 0–1
decisionSTRINGBLOCKED / REVIEW / APPROVE
model_versionSTRINGML platform run ID
scored_atTIMESTAMPPartition column
latency_msINT64Inference latency
🔍explanationsAuto-explain REVIEW/BLOCK
ColumnTypeDescription
transaction_idSTRINGFK → predictions
shap_valuesJSONFeature → SHAP value map
top_driversARRAY<STRING>Top-5 feature names
narrativeSTRINGLLM-generated explanation
base_valueFLOAT64SHAP base (population avg)
rule_traceJSONRules evaluated / fired / final
last_fired_ruleSTRINGRule ID that set final outcome
explained_atTIMESTAMPPartition column
🏷labelsFeedback loop
ColumnTypeDescription
transaction_idSTRINGFK → predictions
labelSTRINGfraud / legit / unknown
analyst_idSTRINGReviewing analyst
confidenceSTRINGhigh / medium / low
notesSTRINGFree-text analyst notes
labeled_atTIMESTAMPPartition column

LN-44821$24,500J. HarringtonPersonal / 60modti_ratio (+0.34)
0.82
HighREVIEW▶
0.82 High REVIEW top: dti_ratio
SHAP Explanation
Narrative

High early-default risk. DTI of 0.61 exceeds 0.43 guideline. Three hard inquiries in 90 days, utilization jumped 38%→74% in 60 days. Long deposit tenure and savings partially offset. Conditional approval at risk-adjusted rate recommended.

SCORE 0.82
RISK TIER High
TOP DRIVER dti_ratio
DECISION REVIEW

Drift Detail —
Distribution Shift (12 weeks)
PSI crossed warning threshold (0.05) in week 7. Investigate the underlying data pipeline for changes around that date.
Reference vs Current Distribution
Blue = training distribution. Orange = production (last 7 days). Shift indicates merchants in a new risk tier have increased volume.
Recommended Actions
merchant_risk_scorePSI = 0.071 — above warning threshold. Re-check merchant risk scoring pipeline for recent changes.
RecalibrationScore distribution has shifted. Consider re-calibrating the model on recent production data.

Override Patterns Last 7 days · 13 overrides
PatternCountLikely Cause
Model: BLOCK → Analyst: APPROVE 7 Rule over-triggered on amount threshold
Model: REVIEW → Analyst: BLOCK 4 Late-stage velocity pattern missed
Rule: REVIEW → Analyst: APPROVE 6 Merchant known to analyst but not model
Rule: BLOCK → Analyst: REVIEW 2 Score borderline, context clears

Overrides are logged and fed back into the active learning loop. Recurring patterns are surfaced as rule optimization candidates in the Decision section.

Disagreement Rate
analyst vs model
Disagreement Analysis by Score Band Where analysts override model/rules
Score BandCases Disagreements RatePattern
0.50–0.65 28 11 39% Thin-file apps; analyst uses bureau notes
0.65–0.75 34 7 21% DTI borderline; context-dependent
0.75–0.85 41 4 10% Strong signals; occasional override
0.85–0.95 29 2 7% Clear declines; rare override

Cases where analyst decision differs from the rule engine final outcome. High disagreement in the 0.55–0.65 band suggests the model uncertainty region may benefit from a new rule or score recalibration.

Ground Truth Pipeline
Analyst labels case (ground truth)
Stored in predictions table with analyst_label field
Label buffer accumulates (312/500)
Retrain triggered at 500 new labels or drift alert
Challenger model trained on updated dataset
Auto-registered as new experiment run
Challenger evaluated vs champion
Promoted if AUC lift > 0.005 with p < 0.05
Agreement Rate (12 weeks)

Weekly analyst–model agreement rate. Declining trend may indicate model drift.

Label Volume (12 weeks)

Cases labeled per week. Dips may reflect analyst capacity or queue throttling.

Est. days to trigger4 days
Last retrain18 days ago
Est. AUC after retrain0.948

Outcomes

Configure decision labels for this project
⚙
Sequential Rule Engine — Last-Fired-Wins
All rules are evaluated in order. The outcome set by the last rule whose condition is true becomes the final decision. If no rules fire, the fallback outcome is used.
FALLBACK
REVIEW
APPROVE
Application proceeds to funding automatically.
Human review requiredNo
Currently in queue0
REVIEW FALLBACK
Application held for underwriter review.
Human review requiredYes
Currently in queue18
DECLINE
Application declined. Adverse action notice sent.
Human review requiredNo
Currently in queue0
ESCALATE
Complex case referred to senior credit officer.
Human review requiredYes
Currently in queue2
Outcome Routing
OutcomeTriggers Human ReviewNotifies CustomerLogged to PredictionsWebhook
APPROVENoYesYes—
REVIEWYes — Ana. QueueNoYes—
BLOCKNoYes — declineYesfraud-alert
ESCALATEYes — SeniorNoYesescalation

Rule Editor

Sequential engine · last-fired-wins
How it works: All rules are evaluated top-to-bottom on every prediction. Each rule that fires sets the outcome. The last rule to fire is the final decision. Drag rows to reorder. Rules with lower priority (higher number) override earlier ones.
Active Rules 5 rules · evaluated in order
Order Name Condition Outcome Frequency Last Fired Status Actions
⠿ 1 Strong auto-approve default_score < 0.35 AND dti_ratio < 0.40 APPROVE 210/day last fired 3 min ago Active
⠿ 2 High DTI review dti_ratio > 0.45 AND default_score > 0.45 REVIEW 88/day last fired 9 min ago Active
⠿ 3 Thin file review credit_history_months < 12 AND default_score > 0.40 REVIEW 34/day last fired 18 min ago Active
⠿ 4 High default-risk block default_score >= 0.75 DECLINE 52/day last fired 6 min ago Active
⠿ 5 Escalate complex default_score >= 0.80 AND employment_type = self_emp ESCALATE 9/day last fired 41 min ago Active
+ Add New Rule

Rule Firing Frequency

Daily firing count per rule. Rules firing rarely may be redundant; rules with high FP may need threshold tuning.

Accuracy by Rule
RuleFrequencyPrimary Outcome TP RateFP RateFN Rate
Strong approve 210/day APPROVE

94%
 5%

TP = rule outcome matches ground truth. FP = rule fires but analyst/ground truth disagrees. FN = rule did not fire but should have.

Score × Outcome Heatmap

Distribution of final rule outcomes across model score bands. Darker cell = higher concentration.

🎯 Optimization Suggestions

Auto-generated by minimizing FP+FN on the evaluation dataset while preserving TP rate ≥ current baseline.

🔴 High — Thin-file review (rule 3) has 34% FP rate
💡 Add credit_history_months > 6 guard; estimated −22% FP
🟡 Med — High DTI rule fires broadly (88/day, 32% FP)
💡 Combine DTI > 0.45 WITH score > 0.55 to sharpen precision
🟢 Low — Strong approve cutoff leaves ~5% good loans in REVIEW
💡 Lower from 0.35 → 0.30 for single-feature DTI < 0.36 cases
